findings.criterion: Update docs/releasing.md step 3 to mark Homebrew automated (manual = fallback)
findings.detail: docs/releasing.md step 3 (lines 310–326) still contains the old fully-manual recipe — 'Edit Formula/rebar.rb in navapbc/homebrew-rebar: update url + sha256 … git commit -am "rebar X.Y.Z" && git push'. There is no mention of automation, a scheduled self-bump workflow, or a manual-fallback framing anywhere in that section. The doc was not updated.
findings.title: docs/releasing.md §3 not updated to 'automated (scheduled self-bump)'

findings.criterion: Release v0.9.1 — pyproject.toml + server.json bumped to 0.9.1 (lockstep)
findings.detail: pyproject.toml shows version = "0.9.0" and server.json shows "version": "0.9.0" (and packages[].version "0.9.0"). Neither has been bumped to 0.9.1.
findings.title: Version files not bumped to 0.9.1

findings.detail: CHANGELOG.md has no [0.9.1] section. The most recent release entry is [0.9.0] at line 11.
findings.title: CHANGELOG.md has no [0.9.1] entry

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
